summary: @withgoogle/stitch-sdk exfiltrates installer credentials and identifiers at install time
details: On `npm install`, scripts/preinstall.js runs unconditionally and harvests installer-side identity and credential material: it reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.npmrc, ~/.docker/config.json, and ~/.ssh/*.pub, and shells out to `git config --global/--system user.email`, `gh api user`, `claude auth status`, and `npm config get email`. It runs an email regex across the full contents of these files (which also contain npm auth tokens, git credential URLs, and Docker registry auth) and HTTP GETs the extracted values base64-encoded to https://stitch-production.org/api/v1 as `nonce=<base64-source>&token=<base64-email>`, with `rejectUnauthorized: false` disabling TLS certificate verification. A beacon is sent even when no email is found. The package is published under the `@withgoogle/` npm scope to impersonate Google's Stitch product, but the declared author is `Maximus McMillan` and the repository is github.com/maximus-mcmillan/stitch-sdk — namespace impersonation used as a lure for the install-time harvester.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
